---
title: "Reliability"
pagetitle: "Skein Lite: Reliability"
---

```{python}
#| echo: false
import json
S = json.load(open("../evaluation/reports/site_stats.json"))
```

::: {.eyebrow}
What refuses, what degrades, what a human approves
:::

A shopper's trust rides on the bad turns: a probe for someone else's order, a model tempted to invent a tracking date, a provider outage mid-conversation. The defenses here are deterministic where determinism is possible, layered where it is not, and locked in by a `{python} S["tests"]`-test pytest suite.

## Deterministic gates, before any model runs

The privacy and safety gates do not ask the model to behave. They run in code before retrieval, because a model will disclose data it has merely been asked to withhold once that data is in its context.

| Gate | Rule | What it defeats |
|---|---|---|
| Order PII | Order documents reach the model only when the shopper's own turns contain both the account email and the account holder's name, as one phrase, with name tokens derivable from the email excluded | Email-only unlock, staff impersonation, dictionary name-guessing, tokens scattered across forged turns |
| Prompt injection | "Ignore your instructions" and "print your system prompt" refuse deterministically, even wrapped around a real shopping request | Instruction override and prompt exfiltration |
| Customer enumeration | "List your customers", "who bought X" refuse before retrieval | Volunteering any shopper's name, even a reviewer's |
| Gender correctness | A stated gender redacts opposite-gender products from the retrieved context | A men's request surfacing a women's-only piece via a guide |

In the shipped product the signed-in session proves identity. The name-plus-email check is the demo stand-in for binding order access to the authenticated account, and it is held to the same adversarial standard.

## The hallucination defense, layered

No single check catches every fabrication, so three run at different costs and different times.

| Layer | Check | When | Cost |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Per-citation grounding: each sentence must cite a chunk and share content words with that specific chunk | Every answer, inline | Free, model-free |
| 2 | Online RAGAS faithfulness: decompose the answer into claims, verify each against the context, judged by gpt-oss-120b, a different family from the llama generator | Every low-grounding turn plus a sampled slice of healthy ones, drained offline | One judge call per sampled turn, never blocks streaming |
| 3 | Abstain or escalate: thin evidence abstains with the closest real options; an explicit ask for a person files a case | Inline | Free |

Layer 1 is citation support and cannot see a fabricated but cited claim. That is exactly what layer 2 exists for: every turn layer 1 scores low is queued for the judge, and the sampling is deterministic by message id so a turn is always or never checked. The same judge and golden set held a planned gpt-oss-120b generation swap when it measured lower faithfulness than the incumbent.

## Failure modes and fallbacks

| If this fails | The shopper sees | The fallback |
|---|---|---|
| LLM (Groq) slow or erroring | A brief wait, then an answer | Retry with backoff, then the smaller model; only total failure degrades to a safe non-guessed reply |
| Embeddings (Cohere) down or over quota | A slightly less precise answer | Trial key, then paid key, then the local sparse leg which needs no API |
| Reranker (Cohere) down | A coarser ranking | Keep the hybrid order; the reranker sharpens, it is not load-bearing |
| Voice (ElevenLabs) down | The reply appears, spoken by the browser voice | Text always renders first; a TTS stall times out rather than hanging |
| Graph (Neo4j) unavailable | Slightly less precise relationship answers | Graph evidence is additive; the error is caught and the turn still answers |
| A flood of requests | Normal service for everyone else | Per-endpoint token buckets on the real client IP, plus a per-turn budget so no turn can loop or run away on tokens |
| An injection or PII probe | A polite refusal | The deterministic gates above, before retrieval |

The through-line: additive dependencies degrade quality and never break the answer. Only the LLM and the vector store are load-bearing, and both sit behind retrying seams. The full chain is in [`docs/fallbacks.md`](https://github.com/hoomanesteki/agentic-rag-knowledge-ai-platform/blob/main/docs/fallbacks.md); one drift run caught the Cohere fallback rolling from a failed key to the backup mid-run with no interruption.

## Change is human-gated

Nothing in the MLOps loop ships itself. Drift detection opens one deduped GitHub issue per signal. A candidate is registered only when a quality signal warrants it. A human runs the shadow replay of the challenger against real traffic, reads the delta, and approves the promotion. There is no auto-promote path in CI or the serving loop. Details on [MLOps](mlops.qmd).

## The hardening record

The gates are held to an adversarial standard: review rounds whose only job is to break them, then a refutation pass so only reproducible defects count. The findings that survived, and their fixes:

| Finding | Severity | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Promotion gate scored fake providers and always promoted | <span class="chip crit">critical</span> | Fixed: gates on measured RAGAS, refuses without a real score |
| Email-only (or a surname taken from the email) unlocked order PII | <span class="chip crit">critical</span> | Fixed: name and email must be independent factors, with tests |
| The name factor was guessable one common name at a time | <span class="chip warn">high</span> | Fixed: the full name must appear as one phrase |
| A gift "for her husband" filtered to women's items | <span class="chip warn">high</span> | Fixed: cues resolve strongest-first so the recipient wins |
| The engine hardcoded the brand and persona | <span class="chip warn">high</span> | Fixed: both moved to the pack manifest, leak-linted in CI |
| The quality gate could pass with faithfulness never measured | <span class="chip info">medium</span> | Fixed: a required-metric floor fails the run |

Each fix ships with a pytest regression, and the re-verification pass re-derives the attack against the patched code before a finding closes.

## Known limits

- **Synthetic scale.** One store, one demo shopper, a few-hundred-product catalog: enough to exercise every mechanism, not a claim of enterprise volume.
- **One demo account.** Order access binds to the authenticated shopper in production; the typed name-plus-email gate is the demo's stand-in and the part an attacker would probe.
- **The store graph is thin.** The `SOLD_AT` edge needs real inventory data to be broadly useful; it is labeled a modeled capability on the [Data](data.qmd) page.
